description: Tempdoc 565 §13 — the spatial-composition single-authority register. A governed multi-zone composition FRAME (the agent window's trace-spine | reading | evidence — distinct CONTENT ZONES, each a projection) is GENERATED from a declared zone-set by the ONE Composition primitive (composeGridStyles, primitives/compositionLayout.ts). The anti-drift teeth are in GENERATION (a registered adopter routes its frame through the primitive, so the hand-authored grid for that content does not exist to drift — 564 'Gate is the floor, not the mechanism'). This register is the CATALOG (which surfaces are governed multi-zone compositions); the gate (check-composition-surfaces.mjs) enforces POSITIVE COVERAGE — every `adopters` entry must actually compose the primitive. It deliberately does NOT scan for arbitrary multi-track grid-template-columns: a governed composition frame is syntactically indistinguishable from a bespoke leaf grid (a label+value pair, a card grid, a toolbar — zone CONTENTS, NOT the governed frame, per the 559 §8 cut), and the ui-web tree has many such legitimate grids (the same lesson as the §3.B status-tone gate). A NEW governed multi-zone composition is a discovery step (add it to `adopters` + route it through the primitive), review-gated, not scan-gated — kernel-consistent with governance/adaptive-regions.v1.json ('adding the row is the discovery step').

primitive.rationale: ONE Composition primitive — composeGridStyles GENERATES the grid frame (responsive grid-template-columns + per-zone placement) as a total function of a declared ZoneDecl[]. It owns the zone FRAME (placement, margin balance, empty-collapse); the zone CONTENTS (a column's internal layout/scroll/measure) stay the component's CSS (the 559 §8 frame/contents cut).
